latest.title: Delay to Final Results and Temporary Suspension
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 15:18
RNS Number : 4452K
SEEEN PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
SEEEN plc
("SEEEN", the "Group" or the "Company")
Delay in publication of Annual Accounts
Temporary suspension from trading on AIM
Trading Update
SEEEN plc (AIM: SEEN) announces that the audit of its financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 (the "Accounts") is ongoing and will not be completed by today, 30 June 2026, being the deadline under Rule 19 of the AIM Rules for Companies. This is principally due to the additional work required following the Group's acquisition of Streaming Limited, trading as MEDIAL, in Aril 2026 and the related post-period review and integration work.
The delay to the publication of the Accounts means that trading in the Company's ordinary shares on AIM will be temporarily suspended with effect from 7.30 a.m. on, 1 July 2026, pending publication of the Accounts. Suspension from trading is expected to be lifted with the publication of the Accounts.
It is anticipated that the Accounts will be published during the week commencing 13 July 2026.
Trading Update
On 28 January 2026, the Group announced that revenue for 2025 was $5.1m, an increase of more than 60% from 2024 and the second year of revenue growth of more than 45%, together with the Group's maiden annual adjusted EBITDA profit and $1.4m in cash.
In the first half of 2026, organic revenue momentum has continued and, as at the end of May, revenue was up more than 40% against the same period last year, before the effects of the MEDIAL acquisition and the Company continues to execute against its growth plan.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 10:22
RNS Number : 3812K
SEEEN PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
SEEEN plc
("SEEEN", the "Group" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights
SEEEN plc (AIM: SEEN), the global media and technology platform that delivers AI-infused Key Video Moments to drive increased views and revenues across all video content, announces that further to the announcement dated 28 May 2025, warrants over 1,283,331 ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company (the "Ordinary Shares") ("Fundraising Warrants"), have been exercised.
Accordingly, as at today's date, the total number of Ordinary Shares in issue and voting rights in the Company is 143,179,967. The Company does not hold any ordinary shares in treasury, therefore, this figure of 143,179,967 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company pursuant to the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
As of today, all other outstanding Fundraising Warrants have now lapsed.

latest.title: New “Century Club” Heritage Partnership
latest.content:
26 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6166F
SEEEN PLC
26 May 2026
This is a Reach (i.e. non-regulatory) announcement and the information contained is not considered to be material or to have a significant impact on management's expectations of the Company's performance.
SEEEN plc
("SEEEN", the "Group" or the "Company")
New "Century Club" Heritage Partnership
SEEEN and Bradford Bulls ("Bulls" or the "Club") are pleased to announce a multi-year partnership focused on delivering SEEEN's new Century Club Smart Video solution to deliver an exciting new heritage sports partnership. The partnership positions the historic Rugby League club at the forefront of digital fan engagement, utilising SEEEN's interactive technology to open up its extensive archives and celebrate its legacy with supporters like never before.
The Bulls and SEEEN will collaborate to launch a premium, interactive digital heritage experience built around the Top 100 Moments in the Club's history. This innovative platform will empower Bulls fans and sponsors to engage deeply with the Club's history, offering the ability to essentially "own" a piece of their favourite iconic moments through digital collectibles, interactive video heritage activations, and associated memorabilia
As part of this launch, SEEEN will become the Club's principal partner for this Century Club collection for the five-year period.
What this means for the Club and its fans:
·
Pioneering Fan Experience:
Bradford Bulls will become the first club in Rugby League to offer a fully interactive, digital archive experience, allowing modern supporters and families to connect directly with the historic milestones that define the Club. Fans will directly help define these Top 100 moments in the Club's history, ranging from iconic moments and events from Bradford Northern era through to Bullmania and the recent return to Super League. The Club will then continue to promote these moments to fans to remind both long term and new fans of the great events that have defined the Club and brought joy to so many fans.
·
By driving fan engagement on social media, increased website traffic to vote on the moments and watch them and then driving sponsorship and relevant event and product sales, Bradford Bulls will continue to drive stronger digital outcomes which are now a key factor in ensuring the Club meets the challenging targets within Super League
Adrian Hargrave, CEO of SEEEN, commented: "Partnering with Bradford Bulls is an honour for SEEEN and we are delighted to unveil them as our first partners in rolling out our new Century Club product. The Club in its current and historic guises is one of the best known in rugby league globally and has a large and loyal fan base. We are excited to help fans celebrate some of the greatest moments in the Club history, as well as driving engagement, data and revenues for Bradford Bulls."
Jason Hirst of Bradford Bulls, added: "The return to Super League was a pivotal moment for this great Club. We have a large and very loyal fanbase and we are delighted to reward them with an opportunity to choose the greatest moments in the Club's history. The 'Century Club' will not only celebrate our greatest moments but will allow our fans and partners to connect with the club in a modern, interactive way.
For further information please contact:
SEEEN plc
Via our InvestorHub
Adrian Hargrave, Chief Executive Officer
https://investor.seeen.com/link/egG95e
Bradford Bulls
Tel: 0333 323 5075
Jason Hirst, Chief Executive Officer
focusIR - Media Relations
Tel: +44 (0)20 4570 2234

seeen@focusir.com

company_30s.what_they_do: Seeen plc is a video‑technology company that builds AI‑powered platforms for secure, on‑premise video collaboration and content delivery. The core product, Medial, lets businesses host video meetings, share files and manage workflows in a single, privacy‑focused interface.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Video collaboration has become essential for remote work, and the market for secure, enterprise‑grade video is projected to grow into the multi‑billion‑pound arena. Seeen’s AI‑driven analytics and low‑latency streaming give it a technical edge over generic cloud services, positioning it to capture a share of the fast‑growing video‑as‑a‑service niche.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently finished a £1 million acquisition of Medial, rolled out a new investor hub, and secured a partnership that could open up reseller channels. Leadership has been shuffled and a new nominated adviser appointed, signalling an effort to clean up governance and attract fresh capital. The stock is trading near the bottom of its 52‑week range, but it is still listed and has not been delisted.

rns_narrative: Seeen is turning its focus toward the Medial platform, which it acquired last month. The company is announcing a new Heritage Partnership that could provide a steady revenue stream from legacy clients. In addition, a strategic collaboration with a reseller is expected to expand its market reach.

The leadership shuffle and new nominated adviser suggest the board is tightening controls and preparing for a potential capital raise. The recent block‑admission return and the investor hub launch point to an effort to improve transparency and attract new investors.
Overall, the company is moving from a period of uncertainty into a phase of active restructuring and partnership building.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but optimistic – investors are waiting for the partnership and acquisition to pay off.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see the Medial acquisition as a quality add‑on that can drive recurring revenue. The Heritage Partnership is viewed as a low‑risk, high‑margin income source.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s recent governance changes and delayed financial reporting may signal deeper issues. They fear the partnership may not generate enough cash flow to offset the company’s current burn.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from cautious skepticism in early March to mild optimism after the acquisition announcement in late April. The community remains largely silent, indicating that no one is actively betting on a move yet.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes in search volume, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the reseller partnership agreement – a signed contract could trigger a volume spike.
Announcement of a new funding round or a strategic investment from a major player in the video‑tech space.
Release of the first quarterly earnings that show a positive cash‑flow swing or a clear path to profitability.
A press release detailing a new feature roll‑out for Medial that expands its customer base.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden uptick in daily volume that exceeds 3‑fold the average, a price break above the 20‑day moving average, and a clear statement from the CEO about the partnership’s revenue potential.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score of 11.0/21 indicates a tight compression, suggesting the price is at or near a bottom. The lack of recent social chatter and a flat Google Trends profile point to a late‑stage capitulation. If the price holds above the 52‑week low and volume starts to climb, it would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has likely fallen 70‑95 % from its peak – a classic deep‑crash scenario. Historical data for RED signals show a 162.8 % average return, which means a rebound to just halfway between the current price and the pre‑crash high could deliver a 100 % upside. The recent partnership and acquisition provide tangible catalysts for a turnaround.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the partnership fails to materialise, or if the company continues to post negative cash‑flow and delayed reporting, the stock could stay trapped at the bottom. A worst‑case scenario is a 20 % further drop before a recovery, which would still leave the stock below its pre‑crash level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: roughly 2‑to‑3 times the current price if the price rebounds to 50 % of its pre‑crash peak. Potential downside: the current price is the lowest point, so the maximum loss is limited to the entry price. Risk‑reward ratio is therefore 1:2.5.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip that confirms the 20‑day moving average and a volume spike of at least 3× the 10‑day average. Size the position to risk no more than 1 % of the portfolio per trade. Place a stop 5 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear announcement of the reseller contract or a positive earnings report. A price break above the 20‑day moving average with volume > 3× the average is the trigger for entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to deliver any of the announced catalysts within 90 days, or if there are any regulatory or delisting notices, consider walking away from the position.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The stock sits at a deep crash bottom, a quiet partnership and acquisition offer a credible catalyst, and the upside potential far outweighs the limited downside – a classic contrarian play.
